title: 第 95 節
en: 4 The Kashmir MS. reads svapna^.
5 The Kashmir MS. reads askandati.
6 The Kashmir MS. reads avagirati.
7 The commentator separates the last dream, so as to bring their number to ten.

Ill ARAiVYAKA, 2 ADHYAYA, 5 KIIAiVDA, 4. 263 him fast, and cook a pot of milk, sacrifice it, accompanying each oblation with a verse of the Ratri hymn (Rv. X, 127), and then, after having fed the Brahma;zas, with other food (prepared at his house) eat himself the (rest of the) oblation.

19. Let him know that the person within all beings, not heard here1, not reached, not thought, not subdued, not seen, not understood, not classed, but hearing, thinking, seeing, classing, sounding, understanding, knowing, is his Self.
Fifth Kha^-da2.
1. Now next the Upanishad of the whole speech. True all these are Upanishads of the whole speech, but this they call so (chiefly).
2. The mute consonants represent the earth, the sibilants the sky, the vowels heaven.
The mute consonants represent Agni (fire), the sibilants air, the vowels the sun.
The mute consonants represent the i?zg-veda, the sibilants the Ya^nr-veda, the vowels the Sama-veda.
The mute consonants represent the eye, the sibilants the ear, the vowels the mind.
The mute consonants represent the up-breathing, the sibilants the down-breathing, the vowels the back-breathing.
3. Next comes this divine lute (the human body, made by the gods). The lute made by man is an imitation of it.
4. As there is a head of this, so there is a head of that (lute, made by man). As there is a stomach 1 The Kashmir MS. reads sa yata^ jruto.
2 After having inserted the preceding chapter on omina and the concluding paragraph on the highest knowledge, he now returns to the meditation on the letters.

of this, so there is the cavity1 (in the board) of that. As there is a tongue of this, so there is a tongue 2 in that. As there are ringers of this, so there are strings of that3. As there are vowels of this, so there are tones of that. As there are consonants of this, so there are touches of that. As this is endowed with sound and firmly strung, so that is endowed with sound and firmly strung. As this is covered with a hairy skin, so that is covered with a hairy skin.
5. Verily, in former times they covered a lute with a hairy skin.
6. He who knows this lute made by the Devas (and meditates on it), is willingly listened to, his glory fills the earth, and wherever they speak Aryan languages, there they know him.
7. Next follows the verse, called vagrasa, the essence of speech. When a man reciting or speaking in an assembly does not please, let him say this verse :

8. ' May the queen of all speech, who is covered, as it were, by the lips, surrounded by teeth, as if by spears, who is a thunderbolt, help me to speak well.' This is the vagrasa, the essence of speech.
Sixth Kha.zv.da.
1. Next Krz'shf/aHarita4 confided this Brahma^a5 concerning speech to him (his pupil) :
1 The Kashmir MS. reads udara evam, &c.
2 Vadanam, what makes the instrument speak, hastena. Coram.
3 Here the order is inverted in the text.
4 One of the sons of Harita, who was dark. Comm.

5 Brahma«a, in the sense of Upanishad, this secret doctrine or explanation. It forms an appendix, like the svish/akr/t at the end of a sacrifice. ' Iva,' which the commentator explains as restrictive or useless, may mean, something like a Brahmawa.

Ill ARAWYAKA, 2 ADHYAYA, 6 KHAiYDA, 8. 265 2. Pra^apati, the year, after having sent forth all creatures, burst. He put himself together again by means of Pandas (Vedas). Because he put himself together again by means of Pandas, therefore (the text of the Veda) is called Sawhita (put together).
3. Of that Sa;;zhita the letter n is the strength, the letter sh the breath and self (atman).
zh: 4 喀什米爾古卷本（Kashmir MS.）作 svapna。
5 喀什米爾寫本讀作askandati。
6 喀什米爾手稿本作 avagirati。
評注者將最後一個夢分開，以便使其數目達到十個。
《阿蘭尼亞卡》第二篇第五章第四節 263 他要緊緊抓住（那頭牛），煮一鍋牛奶，作為祭品獻上，每次獻祭都伴隨誦讀《夜讚歌》(Rv. X, 127) 中的一節經文，然後在用（他家裡準備的）其他食物餵飽婆羅門之後，自己吃掉（剩餘的）祭品。
19. 願他得知存在於一切生命中的那位（person），既未在此處被聞、未被觸及、未被思考、未被制服、未被看見、未被理解、未被分類，然而卻能聞、能思、能見、能分類、能發聲、能理解、能知，此乃其自我（Self）。
第五卡陀（Kha^-da2）
現在接著談整體言語的奧義書。誠然，所有這些都是整體言語的奧義書，但他們主要就稱這個為奧義書。
2. 清濁音代表大地，咝音代表天空，元音代表天堂。
啞音代表阿耆尼（火），齒音代表空氣，元音代表太陽。
清音代表梨俱吠陀（Rig-Veda），擦音代表夜柔吠陀（Yajur-veda），元音代表娑摩吠陀（Sama-veda）。
無聲輔音代表眼睛，咝音代表耳朵，元音代表心靈。
不發聲的子音代表上升的呼吸，咝音代表下降的呼吸，元音代表後向的呼吸。
3. 其次是這件神聖的琵琶（人的身體，由諸神所造）。人所製造的琵琶乃是對它的模仿。
4. 就如同這個有一個頭（琵琶，由人製造），那個也有一個頭。就如同這個有一個胃 1 克什米爾抄本讀作 sa yata^ jruto。
在插入了上述關於徵兆（omina）的章節，以及關於最高知識的結論段落之後，他現在回到對字母的冥想。
如此乎此者，彼亦有其空腔¹（在木板上）。此有其舌²，彼亦有其舌。此有其撥弦者，彼亦有其弦³。此有其元音，彼亦有其音色。此有其輔音，彼亦有其觸感。此既具聲音而絃緊，彼亦具聲音而絃緊。此覆以多毛之皮，彼亦覆以多毛之皮。
5. 誠然，在往昔他們用毛皮覆蓋一把琵琶。
誰若知曉由提婆所製的這支琵琶（並冥想於其上），便會被樂意傾聽，他的榮耀充滿大地，凡是言說雅利安語言之處，人們都認識他。
其次是稱為 vagrasa（言語之精要）的詩句。當一個人在集會中誦讀或發言未能使人滿意時，他應當誦說此詩句：
8. 「願統治一切言語的皇后——她彷彿為唇所覆蓋、如同為矛所圍繞的齒牙所環繞、她是一道雷電——幫助我言語精妙。」這就是瓦格拉薩（vagrasa），言語的精要。
第六部《卡札瓦達》（Kha.zv.da.）
其次，克利斯訥夫哈里塔向他（他的弟子）傾心授以關於言語的婆羅門教義：
克什米爾手稿本作 udara evam 等。
瓦達南（Vadanam），使樂器發聲者，哈斯特納（hastena）。當面。
3 此處文本中的順序是倒轉的。
4 哈里塔（Harita）之子其中之一，膚色黝黑。按語。
梵天（Brahma）在奧義書的意義上，是指這個秘密教義或解釋。它形成了一種附錄，如同犧牲儀式末尾的「自我犧牲」（svish/akr/t）。「伊瓦」（Iva）經評論家解釋為限制性或無用的，可能意指類似梵天的東西。
《阿蘭耶迦》第二篇第六章第八節第265處。梵天（Prajāpati）乃年歲也，既遣出諸生類，遂破裂焉。彼藉由般度（Vedas）而重新聚合自身。因其藉由般度而重新聚合自身，故（吠陀之文本）稱為《森希塔》（Sawhita，意為聚合）。
3. 在那部《梵經集》(Samhita)中，字母n是其力量，字母sh是其氣息與自我(atman)。
